deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: now you re acting like kindergarteners instead of fifth graders not acceptable

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: now you re acting like kindergarteners instead of fifth graders not acceptable okay so tell me about your box

scenes.rationale: Frames students' behavior through a demeaning comparison to younger children rather than describing the specific off-task behavior, characterizing them by falling short of a grade-level standard.

deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: i don t understand why i have some students who really can follow directions and others are not able to follow directions

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: oh student j said oh i know i have another person who said oh i know and you young man you re not learning right now because you re listening to someone else talking while we are having a mathematical discussion

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: all right so some thought student m you re not paying attention student e turn around student m turn your body around whatever you re playing with give that to me you know i don t understand why i have some students who really can follow directions and others are not able to follow directions so what s your thought what s your thought

scenes.rationale: Sorts students into a group defined by a lack ('not able to follow directions') and frames them comparatively against those who can, rather than addressing the task or instructions.

scenes.rationale: Attributes students' difficulty to a character/motivational flaw ('being stubborn') rather than to the demand of the task or gaps in instruction.
